assertions.reason: The new-session UI kept Mission selection independent from workspace mode; Mission stayed selected while Local and New WorkTree toggled normally.
evidence: Opened the real Mission-GUI-TEST new-session entry point via data-testid `new-session-Mission-GUI-TEST`.
DOM/test-id inspection confirmed `session-mode-mission`, `session-mode-local`, and `session-mode-new-worktree` were present together in the same SessionConfigPage.
After selecting Mission and then New WorkTree, `session-mode-mission` still had selected styling (`border-foreground/30 bg-accent/60`) while `session-mode-new-worktree` became selected.
After switching workspace back to Local, `session-mode-mission` remained selected and both workspace options were still available.
Annotated screenshot: /Users/clive/dev/droi/.worktrees/droi--happy-yak-p5n5/.factory/tmp/foundation-routing-session-config.png

assertions.reason: Mission sessions were visually and programmatically distinct in the sidebar, with explicit Mission badges and `session-mission-{sessionId}` targeting.
evidence: Created Mission session `6cbddfbd-ed60-4eeb-9549-7d311be32029` in project `Mission-GUI-TEST` using visible namespace text `foundation-routing mission validation session. Confirm Mission routing only.` and model `CCH-GPT-5.4`.
Sidebar DOM exposed `data-testid="session-mission-6cbddfbd-ed60-4eeb-9549-7d311be32029"` for the created Mission row.
The sidebar row text for that session started with the explicit visible label `Mission`, matching the Mission indicator requirement.
Other Mission sessions in the same project also rendered with `session-mission-*` test ids, confirming consistent Mission targeting.
Annotated screenshot: /Users/clive/dev/droi/.worktrees/droi--happy-yak-p5n5/.factory/tmp/foundation-routing-mission-route.png

assertions.reason: Creating a Mission session opened `/mission` while preserving the shared shell chrome around MissionPage.
evidence: After first send from Mission new-session flow, in-app router state was `/mission` with active session `6cbddfbd-ed60-4eeb-9549-7d311be32029`.
Store inspection at the same moment showed `activeIsMission: true` and `activeSessionKind: "mission"`.
DOM simultaneously contained `[data-testid="mission-page"]` and `[data-testid="sidebar-trigger"]`, proving MissionPage rendered inside the existing app shell rather than in a separate chrome.
Mission content was visible in the shared chat shell with the Mission sidebar row selected.
Annotated screenshot: /Users/clive/dev/droi/.worktrees/droi--happy-yak-p5n5/.factory/tmp/foundation-routing-mission-route.png

assertions.reason: A non-Mission session stayed on `/` and the route guard redirected a direct `/mission` navigation attempt back to `/` without rendering MissionPage.
evidence: Created standard session `f57df244-34af-42ed-85e6-c54d87ccca39` in `Mission-GUI-TEST` with visible text `foundation-routing normal routing validation session.`.
Immediately after normal session creation, router state was `/`, `activeIsMission: false`, `activeSessionKind: "normal"`, and `missionPage: false`.
While that normal session was active, invoking in-app `router.navigate({ to: "/mission" })` left the router at `/` before and after the attempt.
`[data-testid="mission-page"]` stayed absent during the normal-session redirect check, so Mission-only UI did not remain visible.

Annotated screenshots: /Users/clive/dev/droi/.worktrees/droi--happy-yak-p5n5/.factory/tmp/foundation-routing-normal-session.png and /Users/clive/dev/droi/.worktrees/droi--happy-yak-p5n5/.factory/tmp/foundation-routing-direct-mission-redirect.png

assertions.reason: Real project and sidebar entry points routed Mission sessions to `/mission` and normal sessions to `/`, including sidebar reselection and project auto-selection of the latest session.
evidence: Clicked Mission sidebar row `session-mission-6cbddfbd-ed60-4eeb-9549-7d311be32029` and confirmed router `/mission`, `activeIsMission: true`, and `missionPage: true`.
Clicked normal sidebar row `session-f57df244-34af-42ed-85e6-c54d87ccca39` and confirmed router `/`, `activeIsMission: false`, and `missionPage: false`.
Clicked the Mission sidebar row again for reselection and confirmed the app returned to `/mission` for the same Mission session.

Switched to another project (`droi`) and then clicked real project entry `project-Mission-GUI-TEST`; project auto-selection restored the latest normal Mission-GUI-TEST session `f57df244-34af-42ed-85e6-c54d87ccca39` on `/`.
Before any interaction, the live app had already restored an existing Mission-GUI-TEST session on `/mission`, providing latest-session restore evidence for the Mission path.
Annotated screenshot for normal reselection state: /Users/clive/dev/droi/.worktrees/droi--happy-yak-p5n5/.factory/tmp/foundation-routing-reselection-normal.png

frictions: `agent-browser` clicks on the sidebar plus button occasionally targeted the neighboring project row instead of the intended `new-session-Mission-GUI-TEST` control. I resolved this by dispatching a click directly against the exact `[data-testid]` element in the Electron DOM.
The normal-session creation path required setting the pending new-session textarea value through DOM events to ensure the send button enabled reliably in the live Electron view.

Console inspection showed pre-existing renderer noise (a favicon 404 and Base UI button-semantics warnings from `app-sidebar.tsx`), but no additional Mission-routing-specific errors during the tested flows.
